name: agent-tools
description: Run 150+ AI apps via inference.sh CLI - image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, 3D, Twitter automation. Models: FLUX, Veo, Gemini, Grok, Claude, Seedance, OmniHuman, Tavily, Exa, OpenRouter, and many more. Use when running AI apps, generating images/videos, calling LLMs, web search, or automating Twitter. Triggers: inference.sh, infsh, ai model, run ai, serverless ai, ai api, flux, veo, claude api, image generation, video generation, openrouter, tavily, exa search, twitter api, grok
capabilities: Generate images using FLUX, Gemini, Grok, and Seedream models
Create videos with Veo, Seedance, Wan, and OmniHuman
Call LLMs including Claude, Gemini, Kimi via OpenRouter
Perform web search with Tavily and Exa
author: inference-sh-6
securitySummary: This is a documentation skill for the inference.sh CLI platform. All static findings are false positives - the skill contains only documentation with CLI examples. The allowed-tools restriction to Bash(infsh *) limits execution to the official inference.sh CLI only. Network access is to inference.sh API endpoints, and credential handling is through standard CLI authentication.
valueStatement: Run 150+ AI applications including image generation, video creation, LLM calls, and web search directly from the command line without GPU setup.
limitations: Uses inference.sh cloud infrastructure - not local execution
API costs apply based on model usage

useCases.targetUser: Developers and designers needing quick image generation
useCases.title: Generate AI Images from CLI
useCases.description: Create images using FLUX, Gemini, or Grok without local GPU setup

useCases.title: Call Claude and Other LLMs
useCases.description: Access Claude, Gemini, and other LLMs via OpenRouter for text tasks

useCases.title: Automate Twitter Posts
useCases.description: Post tweets, send DMs, and manage Twitter account programmatically

promptTemplates.title: List Available AI Apps
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the agent-tools skill to list all available apps on inference.sh
promptTemplates.scenario: Discover what AI models are available

promptTemplates.title: Generate an Image
promptTemplates.prompt: Use agent-tools to generate an image of a sunset over mountains using the flux model

promptTemplates.title: Call Claude API
promptTemplates.prompt: Use agent-tools to call Claude Sonnet via OpenRouter to explain quantum computing in simple terms

promptTemplates.title: Run Video Generation Task
promptTemplates.prompt: Use agent-tools to generate a video of a drone flying over ocean cliffs using Veo 3, then check the task status

bestPractices: Use --no-wait flag for long-running tasks to avoid timeouts
Save frequently used inputs as JSON files for reuse
Use the infsh app sample command to generate valid input templates
Check task status with infsh task get for async operations
antiPatterns: Do not share your INFSH_API_KEY in public repositories
Avoid running large batch jobs without --no-wait flag
Do not assume all apps support all input formats - check app details first

faq.question: Do I need a GPU to use this skill?
faq.answer: No, all AI models run on inference.sh cloud infrastructure. No local GPU required.

faq.question: Is this skill free to use?
faq.answer: The skill is free to install but inference.sh API usage costs apply based on model usage.

faq.question: Can I use Claude API directly?
faq.answer: Yes, you can call Claude via OpenRouter using infsh app run openrouter/claude-sonnet-45.

faq.question: How do I authenticate?
faq.answer: Run infsh login to authenticate via browser, or set INFSH_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: What models are available?
faq.answer: 150+ models including FLUX, Veo, Claude, Gemini, Grok, Seedance, Tavily, Exa, and more.

faq.question: Can I automate Twitter posts?
faq.answer: Yes, use apps like x/post-tweet, x/dm-send, x/user-follow for Twitter automation.
